answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Honestly just people to hang out with regularly. I moved to Toronto eight months ago and I still don't really have a friend group here, which is kind of embarrassing to admit.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Just having people to text when something funny happens or to grab dinner with spontaneously. Back home I had that without even thinking about it.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Small. Like four or five people max. Big groups make me nervous and I end up not really connecting with anyone.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Slow I think? I don't want to show up somewhere and feel like we're supposed to be best friends immediately. Let it build naturally.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: If there's something to actually do, like an activity or a reason to be there, it takes the pressure off just talking.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm usually the one listening and asking questions. I'm not the loudest person but I'm genuinely interested in people.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Board games, trying new restaurants, walks, low-key stuff. I'm not really into bars or clubs.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekends are pretty open for me. Weeknights are harder because I work late sometimes.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Activity friends mostly. Someone to go hiking with or try that new climbing gym. I have work friends but nobody who's into the same outdoor stuff.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: A consistent crew for weekend activities. My schedule is pretty packed so when I do have free time I want it to count.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Active people who actually show up. I've joined Facebook groups before and half the people flake.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I'm fine jumping in. I don't need a lot of warm-up time.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When people are direct and not overly formal. Just be real.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: Organizer type. I end up being the one who books the thing and coordinates logistics.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Hiking, rock climbing, cycling, occasionally a brewery after. Physical stuff mostly.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Saturday mornings are perfect. Sunday afternoons work too. Weeknights basically never.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Deeper friendships. I have lots of acquaintances but nobody I can really talk to about what's actually going on in my life.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Real conversations. Everything feels surface level. How was your weekend, how's work, whatever.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: A small group of people who are also looking for something more meaningful. Not just a social club.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I'd rather go slow and have it be real than rush it. I've had friendships that burned bright and then disappeared.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When someone else goes first and shares something honest. It kind of gives everyone permission.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I tend to be the one who steers things toward actual depth. People tell me I'm easy to open up to.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Dinner, walks, coffee. Things where you can actually talk. I'm not really looking for activity-based stuff.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekday evenings, like Tuesday or Wednesday. Weekends are hit or miss.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Anything at this point lol. I just got out of a long relationship and my whole social life was basically built around my ex.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Everything. I realized I kind of let my own friendships slide over the last few years.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Welcoming, not cliquey. I'm basically starting from scratch so I need people who are okay with that.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Medium? I want to move forward but I also don't want to overshare with strangers right away.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Humor helps. If people are a little funny and not taking everything super seriously.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm pretty social once I warm up. I can talk to anyone eventually.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: I used to love trying new restaurants and going to markets and stuff. Want to get back into that.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Pretty flexible right now honestly. Most evenings and weekends work.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I work from home and I barely leave my apartment some weeks. I need literally any human contact.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Routine social interaction. Even small talk. Remote work has made me realize how much I depended on casual office stuff.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Something with a recurring schedule so I actually have a reason to get out of the house on a regular basis.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Gradual. I'm a bit rusty socially if I'm being honest.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Knowing what to expect. Like a clear format or agenda for what we're doing.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm kind of in the middle. Not leading, not hiding. I contribute but I don't need to be the center.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Board games, trivia, book club type stuff. Low-key structured activities.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Since I work from home I have some flexibility. Evenings are best, any day really.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Community. Like a consistent group of people I see regularly and actually know.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: That feeling of belonging somewhere. I go to events and meet people and then it just never goes anywhere.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: One that actually sticks together. I've been to a lot of one-off things and I'm tired of starting over.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Consistent and regular. Weekly or biweekly would be ideal.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When people seem genuinely interested in each other, not just going through the motions.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: Social glue I guess. I check in on people, remember things they mentioned, that kind of thing.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Cooking together, watching things, potlucks, casual hangouts at someone's place.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Sunday evenings are almost always free for me. Also Thursday nights usually.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Low pressure hangouts. I have social anxiety and big social commitments really stress me out.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Connection without the anxiety that usually comes with it. Most social things exhaust me.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Small and casual. Where it's okay to be quiet sometimes and nobody makes it weird.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Very slow. I need to feel safe before I open up.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: No pressure to perform or be entertaining. Just existing together is enough at first.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: Observer at first. Then once I trust people I can be pretty funny actually.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Things like museum visits, walks, craft stuff. Anything that gives you something to look at or do so there's no awkward silence.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Saturday afternoons. I need the morning to mentally prepare lol.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Honestly I want both — I want fun activity friends but I also want people I can actually talk to. Is that too much to ask.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Balance. My friend groups are either all party mode or all deep feelings. I want something in the middle.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: A mix of personality types. I don't want everyone to be exactly the same.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Fast-ish. I get bored with too much small talk for too long.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: Good energy. Hard to explain but you can just tell when a group has it.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: The one who gets everyone laughing and also somehow ends up giving advice at 2am.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Concerts, escape rooms, trying weird food spots, road trips if we get close enough.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Flexible, I'm pretty spontaneous. Friday or Saturday evenings usually work.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I'm a physician so my schedule is chaotic. I mostly want to meet people who won't take it personally when I have to cancel sometimes.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Friendships that can handle inconsistency. I've lost friends who couldn't deal with my availability.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Flexible and understanding. Other busy professionals maybe.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Whatever pace works around my shifts honestly.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: People who get that I'm not flaky, just exhausted sometimes.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I'm attentive. I listen well. I'm used to holding space for people.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Long dinners, wine, good conversation. Things I can look forward to after a hard week.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: My schedule rotates but I usually have one or two evenings free per week. Hard to say which ones in advance.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Just casual friends to do stuff with. Nothing too intense.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Someone to go to events with. I hate going to things alone.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Chill, no drama.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Normal I guess.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: If people are friendly and not cliquey.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I go with the flow.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Movies, food, sports bars, that kind of stuff.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: Weekends mostly.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I just relocated from Vancouver for work three months ago. I'm specifically looking for people who want to build an actual friend group, not just LinkedIn connections.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: The easy familiarity of people who've known me for a while. Toronto is great but it's hard to break into socially.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: People who are also somewhat new or looking to expand their circle. We'd all be in the same boat.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: I'm ready to invest. I don't want to spend another winter eating dinner alone in my condo.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: A bit of structure at first. Like an icebreaker or a clear reason we're all there.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: I tend to facilitate. I'll ask questions and make sure everyone feels included.
answers.What kinds of activities do you actually: Exploring the city, food tours, live music, skating in winter. Stuff that feels like I'm actually living here.
answers.What times / days are generally easiest: I can do weeknights after 6:30 or most of Saturday.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Something emotionally real. I'm in my late 30s and I'm tired of friendships that never go below the surface.
answers.What has been missing socially in your l: Vulnerability. Nobody talks about real stuff anymore.
answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: People who are reflective and a bit introspective. Not necessarily intense but willing to go there.
answers.What pace of connection feels comfortabl: Gradual but intentional. I want us to be moving toward something deeper, not just killing time.
answers.In a new group, what helps you feel comf: When someone else takes the risk first. I'll match that energy.
answers.What role do you naturally play in a gro: The deep diver. I ask the questions nobody else asks.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Workout buddy situation or group sport. Social connection through doing something.

answers.What kind of group would genuinely feel: Active, motivated, maybe a bit competitive but in a fun way.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: I want consistent community. The same people, regularly, building something over time.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Something low-key. I'm an introvert and I have limited social energy so I need to spend it wisely.

answers.What kind of connection are you looking: Something real. I don't totally know how to describe it but I feel pretty isolated even though I'm around people all day.
